# Particle Connect

> Python SDK, analytics pipeline, and admin UI for integrating with the Particle Health Platform — a nationwide health information exchange (HIE) for clinical data retrieval across thousands of EHR sources. Includes a Python SDK for the Query Flow API (patient registration, clinical records in CCDA/FHIR R4/flat JSON, Signal/ADT event subscriptions), a DuckDB/BigQuery healthcare analytics pipeline, and a React admin UI for the Management API. Use this for EHR integration, health data interoperability, and clinical analytics workflows.

## Start Here Based on Your Task

- **Building an integration?** → 00-terminology.md, 04-sdk-reference.md, 08-authentication.md, 10-environment-setup.md
- **Debugging an error?** → 00-terminology.md, 09-troubleshooting.md
- **Analyzing clinical data?** → 05-analytics-pipeline.md, 11-flat-data-contract.md
- **Setting up webhooks / Signal?** → 02-api-reference.md (Signal section), 12-notification-data-contract.md
- **Submitting documents to Particle?** → 13-bidirectionality.md, 02-api-reference.md (Document section)
- **Managing projects / service accounts?** → 03-management-api-reference.md, 06-management-ui.md
- **Understanding data models?** → 00-terminology.md, 07-data-models.md

## Documentation

- [Terminology](00-terminology.md): Patient ID disambiguation, confusable field names, environment and credential definitions — READ FIRST if working with multiple ID types
- [Project Overview](01-project-overview.md): Repository structure, three sub-projects, and how they relate
- [API Reference — Query Flow](02-api-reference.md): Endpoints for patient registration, clinical data queries, data retrieval (flat/CCDA/FHIR), and Signal (subscriptions, ADT events, webhooks)
- [API Reference — Management](03-management-api-reference.md): Endpoints for projects, service accounts, credentials, and IAM policies
- [SDK Reference](04-sdk-reference.md): Python SDK architecture — modules (patient, query, document, signal), classes, and usage patterns
- [Analytics Pipeline](05-analytics-pipeline.md): DuckDB and BigQuery pipeline for Particle flat data with 15 pre-built SQL queries
- [Management UI](06-management-ui.md): React + FastAPI admin interface architecture and Docker setup
- [Data Models](07-data-models.md): Pydantic schemas for patients, queries, documents, signal/webhooks, and API responses
- [Authentication](08-authentication.md): JWT auth flows for Query Flow API and Management API
- [Troubleshooting](09-troubleshooting.md): Common errors, gotchas, and sandbox limitations
- [Environment Setup](10-environment-setup.md): Prerequisites, credentials, configuration variables, and verification
- [Flat Data Contract](11-flat-data-contract.md): ODCS v3 data contract for flattened clinical data — 22 table schemas, column definitions, types, relationships, and provenance model
- [Notification Data Contract](12-notification-data-contract.md): CloudEvents 1.0 webhook notification schemas — 7 notification types with payload definitions, examples, and signature verification
- [Bi-Directionality](13-bidirectionality.md): Documents API for sending clinical data back to Particle — submit, retrieve, delete, and list documents (CCDA XML/PDF), code value sets, and workflow diagrams

## Instructions

- Always use the Python SDK (`from particle.patient import PatientService`) over raw HTTP calls for new integrations
- ALWAYS use two-letter state abbreviations ("MA") for address_state in patient registration — NEVER use full state names ("Massachusetts")
- Never use the FHIR endpoint in sandbox — it returns 404; use flat or CCDA instead
- Never poll query status in a tight loop — always use exponential backoff (SDK handles this automatically)
- Prefer flat format over CCDA for data analysis and pipeline work; use CCDA when you need original clinical documents
- For bi-directional document submission (sending data back to Particle), see `13-bidirectionality.md` — uses v1 endpoint, requires external patient_id
- The Management API uses a different base URL (`management.*` subdomain) than the Query Flow API
- Auth is NOT OAuth2 — it's a custom flow with GET /auth and custom headers
- Signal subscribe returns 400 when already subscribed — treat as success, not error
- Signal trigger-sandbox-workflow returns plain text "success", NOT JSON — parse accordingly
- Signal webhook notifications use CloudEvents 1.0 format (type: "com.particlehealth.api.v2.transitionalerts")

## Optional

- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Universal agent instructions — build commands, code style, and conventions
